questions.q: The British Raj began after which major Indian uprising?
questions.explanation: Direct Crown rule, the British Raj, began in the aftermath of the Revolt of 1857.

questions.q: Which Act of 1858 transferred control of India to the British Crown?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act 1858 ended Company rule and began direct Crown rule.

questions.q: Before the Raj, India had been governed by which body?
questions.explanation: The British East India Company administered much of India before the Crown took over in 1858.

questions.q: Which British monarch was proclaimed Empress of India in 1877?
questions.explanation: Queen Victoria was proclaimed Empress of India in 1877.

questions.q: The senior British official who governed India on behalf of the Crown was called the what?
questions.explanation: The Crown's chief representative in India was titled the Viceroy and Governor-General.

questions.q: The British Raj formally ended in which year?
questions.explanation: The British Raj ended with Indian independence in 1947.

questions.q: The word 'Raj' in 'British Raj' means what?
questions.explanation: 'Raj' is a Hindi word meaning rule or reign.

questions.q: The grand ceremonial assemblies held to display imperial authority during the Raj were called what?
questions.explanation: Imperial Durbars were grand ceremonies, the most famous being the Delhi Durbars.

questions.q: Under the British Raj, the capital of British India was moved in 1911 from Calcutta to which city?
questions.explanation: The capital was shifted from Calcutta to Delhi in 1911.

questions.q: The 1858 transfer of power to the Crown was accompanied by a famous proclamation issued in the name of which monarch?
questions.explanation: Queen Victoria's Proclamation of 1858 announced the principles of the new Crown government.

questions.q: Who was the first Viceroy of India under the British Raj?
questions.explanation: Lord Canning became the first Viceroy of India in 1858.

questions.q: The London-based minister responsible for Indian affairs during the Raj was titled what?
questions.explanation: The Secretary of State for India in London oversaw Indian administration after 1858.

questions.q: Which 1877 ceremony in Delhi proclaimed Queen Victoria Empress of India?
questions.explanation: The Imperial Assemblage of 1877, organized by Lord Lytton, proclaimed Victoria's new title.

questions.q: The Royal Titles Act of 1876 conferred the title 'Empress of India' on Queen Victoria under which British Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Benjamin Disraeli's government passed the Royal Titles Act of 1876 making Victoria Empress of India.

questions.q: During the Raj, the provincial elite Indian civil servants belonged to which prestigious service?
questions.explanation: The Indian Civil Service (ICS) was the elite administrative corps of the Raj.

questions.q: The 1858 Act abolished which Company body that had shared control of Indian affairs?
options: The Board of Control and Court of Directors
questions.explanation: The Act dissolved the Company's Court of Directors and the Board of Control in favour of the new India Office.

questions.q: Which 1911 event marked the visit of King George V to India during the Raj?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Durbar of 1911 honoured the visiting King-Emperor George V, who also announced the capital's shift to Delhi.

questions.q: Under the Raj, princely states ruled by Indian princes were bound to the Crown through what relationship?
questions.explanation: The doctrine of paramountcy made the Crown the supreme authority over the princely states.

questions.q: Which 1935 Act was one of the last major constitutional reforms enacted during the Raj?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act 1935 introduced provincial autonomy and was a key late reform of the Raj.

questions.q: The British Raj ruled directly over roughly what proportion of the subcontinent's territory, with the rest under princely states?
questions.explanation: British India directly governed roughly three-fifths of the subcontinent; the rest was princely states under paramountcy.

questions.q: The summer capital of the British Raj, where the government moved during hot months, was which hill station?
questions.explanation: Simla (Shimla) served as the summer capital of the British Raj.

questions.q: Which London-based department administered Indian affairs after the 1858 transfer?
questions.explanation: The India Office in London was created to manage Indian administration under the Secretary of State for India.

questions.q: Queen Victoria's Proclamation transferring power to the Crown was read out in India in which month and year?
questions.explanation: Victoria's Proclamation was publicly read in India in November 1858, notably at Allahabad.

questions.q: The Government of India Act 1858 created a new advisory body in London called what?
questions.explanation: The Act established the Council of India to advise the Secretary of State for India in London.

questions.q: Which Viceroy presided over the 1877 proclamation of Victoria as Empress of India?
questions.explanation: Lord Lytton was Viceroy when the Imperial Assemblage of 1877 proclaimed Victoria Empress.

questions.q: The Indian Councils Act of which year first introduced a limited Indian advisory presence in the legislative process under the Raj?
questions.explanation: The Indian Councils Act 1861 allowed Indians to be nominated to legislative councils, a first step under the Raj.

questions.q: The Morley-Minto Reforms, which expanded Indian participation under the Raj, were enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian Councils Act of 1909, the Morley-Minto Reforms, expanded the legislative councils and introduced separate electorates.

questions.q: Which 1919 Act, the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms, introduced 'dyarchy' in the provinces of British India?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act 1919 (Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms) introduced dyarchy in the provinces.

questions.q: Lord Mountbatten, the last person to hold supreme authority for the Crown in India, served as Viceroy and then briefly in what role after August 1947?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten became the first Governor-General of independent India after the Raj ended in 1947.

questions.q: The Indian Independence Act that wound up the British Raj was passed by the British Parliament in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act 1947 provided for the end of the Raj and the creation of India and Pakistan.
